# [doc = " \\[Declared\\] Fill `buf` with bytes from the OS entropy source (`/dev/urandom`)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Never-silent: returns `Err(EntropyError::Unavailable(...))` on any failure (open error,"] # [doc = " short read, EOF, or platform without `/dev/urandom`). Never panics. Never zero-fills"] # [doc = " silently (G2)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `Declared` — source is the kernel CSPRNG via `/dev/urandom`, a genuine OS entropy source,"] # [doc = " but no statistical quality audit has been conducted. Promotion to `Empirical` requires"] # [doc = " documented trials with measured statistical quality (VR-5). Do not use for"] # [doc = " security-sensitive purposes until retagged."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Platform"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Unix/Linux/macOS only. Returns `Err(EntropyError::Unavailable(...))` on platforms that"] # [doc = " do not expose `/dev/urandom`. Use the `getrandom` crate (FLAG-GETRANDOM) for"] # [doc = " cross-platform OS entropy."] pub fn fill_bytes (buf : & mut [u8]) -> Result < () , EntropyError > { if buf . is_empty () { return Ok (()) ; } let mut f = std :: fs :: File :: open ("/dev/urandom") . map_err (| e | EntropyError :: Unavailable (format ! ("open /dev/urandom: {e}"))) ? ; f . read_exact (buf) . map_err (| e | EntropyError :: Unavailable (format ! ("read /dev/urandom: {e}"))) }

# [cfg (test)] mod tests { use super :: * ; # [doc = " Skip helper: treat `EntropyError::Unavailable` as a graceful skip (the repo idiom for"] # [doc = " platform-absent resources — mirrors the `ToolchainMissing` skip in the mlir differential)."] # [doc = " Non-Unix platforms (Windows, WASI, bare-metal) return `Unavailable`; that is correct"] # [doc = " behaviour and must not be treated as a test failure."] macro_rules ! skip_if_unavailable { ($ result : expr) => { match $ result { Ok (v) => v , Err (EntropyError :: Unavailable (_)) => return , } } ; } # [test] fn fill_bytes_32_succeeds_and_fills () { let mut buf = [0u8 ; 32] ; skip_if_unavailable ! (fill_bytes (& mut buf)) ; assert_ne ! (buf , [0u8 ; 32] , "fill_bytes returned all-zero bytes (smoke check)") ; } # [test] fn fill_bytes_empty_ok () { let mut buf : [u8 ; 0] = [] ; let result = fill_bytes (& mut buf) ; assert ! (result . is_ok () , "fill_bytes([]) returned Err: {result:?}") ; } # [test] fn fill_bytes_large_succeeds () { let mut buf = vec ! [0u8 ; 4096] ; skip_if_unavailable ! (fill_bytes (& mut buf)) ; } # [doc = " Smoke test: two successive fills succeed when the entropy source is available."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " This test verifies only that both calls succeed (availability) — not that the two"] # [doc = " buffers differ. A content-quality / non-determinism check (two buffers should differ)"] # [doc = " is an inherently probabilistic assertion; it belongs in a documented statistical audit"] # [doc = " (e.g. Diehard/TestU01 with a recorded pass/fail table), not a unit test. Honest label:"] # [doc = " `Declared` quality, not `Empirical` (VR-5)."] # [test] fn fill_bytes_successive_succeed_when_available () { let mut buf1 = [0u8 ; 32] ; let mut buf2 = [0u8 ; 32] ; skip_if_unavailable ! (fill_bytes (& mut buf1)) ; skip_if_unavailable ! (fill_bytes (& mut buf2)) ; } # [test] fn entropy_error_display () { let e = EntropyError :: Unavailable ("test reason" . to_string ()) ; assert_eq ! (e . to_string () , "entropy unavailable: test reason") ; } # [doc = " Verify the EntropyError type implements std::error::Error (required by public API)."] # [test] fn entropy_error_is_std_error () { let e : Box < dyn std :: error :: Error > = Box :: new (EntropyError :: Unavailable ("x" . to_string ())) ; assert ! (e . to_string () . contains ("entropy unavailable")) ; } }
